_comment: Declared half of the Leiden-aligned Tool & Computational Resource Disclosure. The machine-derivable half (trained base models, MCP servers) is read directly from training/adapters/registry.jsonl and .mcp.json by tools/build_tool_disclosure.py. This file records what is NOT machine-derivable: AI assistants used during development, proprietary judge/inference services used for measurement, and metered compute providers. Keep it honest and current; docs/TOOL-DISCLOSURE.md is regenerated from it and drift-checked in CI.

development_assistants.name: Claude (Anthropic)
development_assistants.role: code authoring, review, and documentation under human direction
development_assistants.note: AI assistants are tools; authorship and responsibility for all results remain with the human maintainer.

development_assistants.name: GitHub Copilot
development_assistants.role: code review and suggestions on pull requests

measurement_services.name: OpenRouter
measurement_services.role: hosted inference for LLM-judge families used in evaluation
measurement_services.note: Used only to grade aggregates; raw judge prompts in held-out sets are never published.

measurement_services.name: LLMHub
measurement_services.role: second independent inference provider for the >=2-judge-family rule

compute_providers.name: RunPod
compute_providers.role: metered GPU jobs for training/eval, driven exclusively through GitHub Actions
compute_providers.note: The only metered compute path; owned hardware (DGX Spark / Mac Studio) is preferred where possible.
